acvp_ref_note: Upstream ACVP-Server ref this attestation was generated against (immutable NIST release tag v1.1.0.42, published 2026-04-16, upstream commit 15c0f3d...). The per-algorithm source_url entries below embed this ref as a human-browseable URL that resolves to the exact attested bytes. .github/workflows/acvp_validation.yml sets ACVP_REF to the same tag and fails the build if this field, the workflow env var, and the value nist_vectors/fetch_vectors.py actually used (recorded in nist_vectors/validation_summary.json::acvp_ref) ever disagree. When refreshing this attestation against a newer ACVP-Server release, update this field, every source_url below, the workflow's ACVP_REF default, and nist_vectors/fetch_vectors.py::DEFAULT_ACVP_REF in the same commit; if the per-algorithm vector counts change with the new snapshot, also update totals and EXPECTED_VECTORS.

algorithms.name: SHA3-256
algorithms.coverage_note: 151 AFT byte-aligned vectors + 100 MCT vectors (1 tcId x 100 outer iterations per FIPS 202 MCT spec).

algorithms.name: SHA3-512
algorithms.coverage_note: 86 AFT byte-aligned vectors + 100 MCT vectors (1 tcId x 100 outer iterations per FIPS 202 MCT spec).

algorithms.name: SHAKE-128
algorithms.coverage_note: 174 AFT byte-aligned vectors + 100 MCT vectors (variable output length, rightmost-16-bits feedback per FIPS 202 MCT spec).

algorithms.name: SHAKE-256
algorithms.coverage_note: 143 AFT byte-aligned vectors + 100 MCT vectors (variable output length per FIPS 202 MCT spec).

total_vectors_skipped_note: total_vectors_skipped = total_vectors_skipped_aft_filtered + total_vectors_skipped_non_aft. The AFT-filtered bucket (run_vectors.py::results.summary.total_skipped, aggregated from per-algorithm vectors_skipped) counts individual vectors filtered out within AFT test groups: non-byte-aligned inputs, non-target parameter sets, ML-KEM encapsulation (AMA does not expose the randomness parameter m), and ML-DSA/SLH-DSA internal/pre-hash test groups. The non-AFT bucket counts entire test groups with testType != 'AFT' that are skipped wholesale (LDT + VOT in the current coverage; MCT moved OUT of this bucket in v2.1.6 when the four SHA-3 MCT runners were added, dropping the count from 1036 to 1032). These are tracked per-algorithm in run_vectors.py under the legacy field name 'mct_skipped' (the field name predates MCT coverage and is now semantically a misnomer — it counts non-AFT test groups generally) and surfaced in validation_summary.json as total_non_aft_skipped (with mct_skipped kept alongside as a backwards-compatible alias for the raw harness field).
skip_categories_aft_filtered: Non-target parameter sets (ML-KEM-512/768, ML-DSA-44/87, SLH-DSA non-SHA2-256f)
ML-KEM encapsulation (randomness parameter m not exposed by AMA API)
ML-DSA/SLH-DSA internal and pre-hash test groups
skip_categories_non_aft: Large Data Test (LDT) groups — multi-gigabyte inputs outside the CI harness scope (8 SHA-3 tcIds)
Variable Output Test (VOT) groups — output-length coverage is already exercised by AFT vectors in the same upstream vector files (1024 SHAKE tcIds)
Any other testType != 'AFT' test group encountered upstream

coverage_changelog.change: Added Monte Carlo Test (MCT) coverage for SHA3-256, SHA3-512, SHAKE-128, SHAKE-256 (400 vectors across 4 algorithms). total_vectors_tested: 815 -> 1215. total_vectors_skipped_non_aft: 1036 -> 1032 (MCT groups moved from skipped to tested; LDT and VOT remain skipped). MCT runner implementation is in nist_vectors/run_vectors.py::_run_sha3_mct and _run_shake_mct.

test_environment.build: CMake Release, -DAMA_USE_NATIVE_PQC=ON, LTO enabled, AVX2 enabled
test_environment.pqc_backend: native C (no liboqs, no PQClean, no third-party PQC dependency)

disclaimers: This is a self-attestation, NOT a CAVP validation certificate.
This document does NOT represent NIST endorsement.
No CAVP, CMVP, or FIPS 140-3 compliance is claimed.
This document is NOT a substitute for an independent cryptographic audit.
Side-channel resistance (timing, power, EM, cache) is out of scope.
Customers in regulated environments requiring FIPS validation must obtain formal CAVP/CMVP validation through an accredited CST laboratory.
